content.full_text:
Flrn Porrucar PnacrrcBs CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95g12_0g07
(9t6) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-088 6
June 25,2001
Alice Reed
Office of the City Clerk
City of Moreno Valley
14177 Frederick Street
Moreno Valley, CA92553
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-148
Dear Ms. Reed:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since you are not requesting adiice on tt . duties of a specific
public official, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c).2
QUESTION
The City of Moreno Valley is holding a ballot measure election on July 31, 2001. The
election is for the purpose of establishing a library parcel fee and the ballot designation is
Measure A. Since the first and second pre-electionstatements include the reporting period
covered by the semi-annual statement due July 3l,z}}l,may cornmittees formed to support or
oppose this measure combine their semi-annual statement with their second pre-election
statement?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Any committee formed prior to June 30, 2001, for the purpose of supporting or
opposing your July 37,2001, measure, may combine the semi-annual statement wilh the second
pre-election statement. This combined statement must be filed no later than July 19, 2001, the
deadline for the second pre-election statement.
lGovernment Code Section 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attttle Z,sections 1g109-1g995,
of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 1g329(cx3).)


Advice Letter No. I-01-148
Page Two
ANALYSIS
Committees formed to support or oppose a ballot measure are r'bquired to file trvo
campaign statements disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with
that election. These pre-election campaign statements are generally filed 40 days before the
election and again 12 days before the election. (Section 84200.8.) Additionally, ba1ot meas,re
committees must file quarterly statements and semi-annual statements. Semi-annual statements
are filed January 31 and July 31, respectively. (Section }4ZOO.)
The City of Moreno Valley is holding an election on July 3 1 , 2001 . The first pre-election
statement filed in connection with this election is due June 21, 2OOl, and covers the reporting
period of January 1 through June 16. The second pre-election statement is due July 19, 2OOl,
and covers the reporting period of June 17 through July 14, 2001. Any ballot measure committee
formed prior to June 30, 200J, would also be required to file'a serhi:anntial statement by July 31
covering the period June 17 through June 30. 
:
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the combining of statements when an
overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an
additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.) It appears
that combining the second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement wouldte
appropriate for the city of Moreno valley's July 3r,2001, election.
AccordinglY, ffiY committee which came into existence prior to June 30, 2OOl, formed to
support or oppose the July 31,2001, ballot measure may combine its semi-annual statement with
its second pre-election statement. The date for filing this combined statement is July lg, Z0Ol,
the date the second pre-election statement is due. A campaign statement filing schedule that
outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for your convenience.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 323-
6935.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Dixie Howard
Manager, Filing Officer Programs
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
